name: Node.js Patterns & Gotchas
description: Common Node.js patterns, gotchas, and best practices

learnings.title: Unhandled promise rejections crash Node.js
learnings.content: Starting from Node.js 15+, unhandled promise rejections terminate the process. Always use try/catch with async/await, or .catch() with promises. Add a process-level handler for unexpected rejections: `process.on('unhandledRejection', handler)`

learnings.title: Use 'node:' prefix for built-in modules
learnings.content: Import Node.js built-in modules with the 'node:' prefix (e.g., `import fs from 'node:fs'`). This makes it explicit that you're using a built-in, prevents conflicts with npm packages of the same name, and is required for some modules like 'node:test'.

learnings.title: fs.promises API for async file operations
learnings.content: Use `import fs from 'node:fs/promises'` for promise-based file operations instead of callbacks. It's cleaner with async/await and avoids callback hell. The synchronous variants (fs.readFileSync) block the event loop.

learnings.title: ESM requires file extensions in imports
learnings.content: When using ES modules (type: 'module' in package.json), you must include file extensions in relative imports: `import { foo } from './bar.js'`. This applies even to TypeScript files when targeting ESM (import .js, not .ts).

learnings.title: __dirname and __filename don't exist in ESM
learnings.content: ES modules don't have __dirname and __filename. Use `import.meta.url` with URL/path utilities instead: `const __filename = fileURLToPath(import.meta.url); const __dirname = path.dirname(__filename);`

learnings.title: Environment variables are always strings
learnings.content: process.env values are always strings or undefined. Never compare directly to numbers or booleans. Use explicit parsing: `parseInt(process.env.PORT, 10)` or `process.env.DEBUG === 'true'`. Consider using a config validation library like zod.

learnings.title: Use AbortController for cancellable operations
learnings.content: AbortController provides a standard way to cancel async operations. Pass the signal to fetch, timers, streams, and custom async functions. Check `signal.aborted` or listen to 'abort' event to handle cancellation gracefully.

learnings.title: Stream pipelines should use pipeline() for error handling
learnings.content: Use `stream.pipeline()` instead of `.pipe()` for proper error handling and cleanup. pipeline() forwards errors and cleans up all streams on failure. Available as promise: `import { pipeline } from 'node:stream/promises'`

learnings.title: Child process stdio: 'inherit' for interactive commands
learnings.content: When spawning processes that need terminal interaction (e.g., prompts, colors), use `stdio: 'inherit'` to connect to parent's stdio. For capturing output programmatically, use 'pipe' and handle stdout/stderr events.

learnings.title: Graceful shutdown: handle SIGTERM and SIGINT
learnings.content: Always handle shutdown signals for production apps: `process.on('SIGTERM', gracefulShutdown)`. Close servers, database connections, and finish pending work before calling `process.exit()`. Docker sends SIGTERM by default.
